title: Container name resolution does not work on `--internal` bridge network
body:
### Issue Description

`--internal` option of `podman network create` states `aardvark-dns` will perform container name resolution, but in my testing container name resolution doesn't actually work.

Note: if I query the DNS server from host, container name resolution does work. So it appears that the DNS server is setup correctly, but the containers cannot reach it.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

***Must be root. Rootless seems unaffected.***
 
1. Create internal bridge network: `podman network create net-internal --internal`
2. Start container `c1` and hold it open: `podman run --rm -d --name c1 --network net-internal fedora sleep 600`
3. Start container `c2` and try to resolve `c1`: `podman run --rm --name c2 --network net-internal fedora curl -sS c1`

### Describe the results you received

`c1` fails to resolve: `curl: (6) Could not resolve host: c1`

### Describe the results you expected

`c1` resolves. More specifically curl should error with `curl: (7) Failed to connect to c1 port 80 after 0 ms: Could not connect to server`.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I've also tried this on:

- Another Arch Linux box with podman-6.0.1: same result
- An EL9 box with podman-5.8.2: same result

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
dup of https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/1055 I guess

You must add a firewall rule yourself to accept the dns traffic on the hos then.
